what_it_does: Acceptance test for Convergence X3: it mints a build-intent at a real corpus address, reloads the persisted record from disk through a fresh Suite, and asserts the payload carries a bounded, deduplicated, JSON-clean `context` bundle produced by the existing R2 gather while preserving address, symbols, and existing payload fields.
observations: Long docstring states the principle, defect, fix, proof model, and completion criteria.
Uses the real fixture address ui://chat/input and expects symbols [code://RhmChat, code://suite/chat].
Attaches 60 bulk annotations to overflow Suite.R2_BUDGET and a low-scored item at a parent address to prove the cap drops it.
Reloads the surfaced record from disk via a fresh FsStore/Suite to verify persistence.
Asserts the mint comment appears exactly once across annotation/chat/event (X8 dedup).
Asserts no `_raw` field leaks and every item has only public keys {kind,address,ts,text,pinned}.
Checks that a bare `surface_build_intent` with no address persists no `context` key (additive-optional).
Verifies `_resolve_context_at` still returns a bounded string block for the existing chat path.
standouts: The BOUNDED keystone uses a deliberately dropped low-scored item as proof the cap actually pruned, not just a size check.
Reload-from-disk via a fresh Suite is the load-bearing proof that the bundle reaches persistent storage.
The additive-optional `context` key is explicitly tested with a negative case for bare intents.
conventions: Uses a global PASS counter and a local `check(label, cond)` helper for self-reporting assertions.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 and constructs a temporary FsStore root for isolation.
Imports from store.fs_store, runtime.registry, and runtime.suite.
Names constants in ALL_CAPS and uses descriptive markers like LOWSCORE_MARK and PREEXISTING.
concerns: Global mutable PASS counter and helper make the test stateful and harder to parallelize.
Hard-coded real corpus address and symbol expectations may break if fixture carving changes.
60 annotation loop plus repeated Suite construction could be slow for an acceptance test.
The chat-path budget allowance adds an arbitrary +400 header buffer to the R2_BUDGET check.
No explicit cleanup of the temporary store root.
notes: An acceptance test that proves build-intents minted at an address inherit the bounded R2 notebook as a persisted `context` bundle, reloaded from disk.
questions: What is the numeric value of Suite.R2_BUDGET?
How exactly do `_r2_gather` and `_r2_score_and_cap` score proximity, recency, and pin decay?
What public fields does `_r2_gather` emit and how is `_raw` stripped?
How does `surface_intent_at` insert the mint comment before gathering context?
What is the X5 precursor and how will the consent-time bundle be consumed downstream?
How does `_resolve_context_at` format the bounded string block for the chat path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it verifies that the existing R2 gathered context is persisted into the build-intent payload at mint time, bounded, deduped, JSON-clean, and additive-optional.
apparent_themes: consent-time trust and auditability of the context bundle
bounded information retrieval with explicit drop-proof
additive schema changes without breaking existing records
backward compatibility of the chat context path
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that the build record captures the same address-bound notebook the chat uses, so later composition can rely on an auditable, capped context rather than re-gathering or dumping everything.
novelty: It turns the budget cap into a falsifiable drop-proof by asserting a specific low-scored parent-address item is absent, and it uses a fresh-Suite reload as the primary proof of persistence.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that defines the X3 contract for persisted context bundles, covering boundedness, deduplication, JSON-clean shape, additive optionality, preservation of existing payload fields, and reload-from-disk validation.
summary_for_embedding: tests/conv_context_acceptance.py is an acceptance test that mints a build-intent at ui://chat/input, reloads the record from disk through a fresh Suite, and asserts the payload contains a bounded, deduped, JSON-clean `context` bundle drawn from the existing R2 gather while preserving address, symbols, and existing fields.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prove that the accumulated notebook at an address is captured at consent/mint time and inherited by the surfaced build record, using the same R2 budget and dedup logic as the chat path.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: A simple test assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test; it raises an AssertionError with a FAIL label when the condition is false, otherwise prints the label and increments the global PASS counter so the test self-reports how many checks passed.
